content:
@flint v0 hybrid
G: split_monolith_into_bounded_services_preserve_order_to_payment_consistency
C: django_monolith ∧ domains("orders,billing,fulfillment,inventory") ∧ team("8_eng") ∧ stack("postgres+kafka+redis+k8s") ∧ pain("release_coupling,merge_conflicts,12min_tests,migration_blocked_deploys") ∧ budget("6mo") ∧ runway("2y") ∧ no_distributed_transactions
P: four_services_one_per_domain ∧ order_service_owns_order_aggregate_and_payment_intent_state ∧ billing_service_owns_payment_methods_invoices_ledger ∧ fulfillment_service_owns_shipments_pick_pack ∧ inventory_service_owns_stock_levels_and_reservations ∧ sync_grpc_on_order_to_payment_critical_path("order→billing.authorize") ∧ async_kafka_everywhere_else("order.created,payment.authorized,payment.captured,inventory.reserved,shipment.dispatched") ∧ order_service_runs_saga_orchestrator ∧ compensating_actions_replace_2pc("void_auth,release_reservation,cancel_order") ∧ each_service_owns_its_postgres_schema_no_cross_service_joins ∧ read_models_via_kafka_projections ∧ outbox_pattern_for_atomic_db_plus_publish ∧ idempotency_keys_on_all_writes ∧ inventory_reservation_ttl("15min") ∧ redis_for_reservation_cache_and_rate_limits ∧ api_gateway_fronts_all_sync_traffic ∧ schema_registry_for_kafka_contracts("avro_or_protobuf") ∧ strangler_fig_migration ∧ per_service_ci_and_deploy ∧ per_service_k8s_namespace
V: order_to_payment_p99_under_500ms ∧ zero_distributed_tx ∧ saga_recovers_from_any_single_service_failure ∧ each_service_test_suite_under_3min ∧ independent_deploys_no_coordination ∧ no_cross_service_db_access ∧ kafka_consumer_lag_alerts ∧ synthetic_order_flow_passes_in_staging_and_prod
A: month1_extract_inventory_behind_facade_dual_write_then_cut ∧ month2_extract_fulfillment_consume_order_events_only ∧ month3_extract_billing_grpc_authorize_plus_kafka_capture ∧ month4_carve_order_service_from_monolith_shell ∧ month5_delete_monolith_harden_sagas_chaos_test ∧ month6_slo_bakeoff_cutover_complete ∧ assign_one_tech_lead_per_service("2_eng_each_rotating_platform_pair") ∧ platform_pair_owns_kafka_outbox_lib_gateway_observability

@flint v0 hybrid
G: define_exclusive_table_ownership_and_cross_service_read_and_event_contracts
C: four_services("order,billing,fulfillment,inventory") ∧ one_owner_per_table ∧ no_cross_service_joins ∧ no_distributed_tx ∧ kafka_plus_grpc_only ∧ customers_is_shared_reference_data
P: order_service_owns("orders,order_items,order_state_log,saga_instances,order_outbox") ∧ billing_service_owns("payments,payment_intents,payment_methods,invoices,ledger_entries,billing_outbox") ∧ fulfillment_service_owns("shipments,shipment_items,shipment_events,carrier_labels,fulfillment_outbox") ∧ inventory_service_owns("stock_levels,inventory_holds,stock_movements,inventory_outbox") ∧ customer_service_owns("customers,customer_addresses,customer_outbox") ∧ order_reads_from_customer("customer_profile_snapshot_via_grpc_on_checkout_plus_kafka_projection_for_lookups") ∧ order_reads_from_inventory("reservation_result_via_grpc_sync_reserve_call") ∧ order_reads_from_billing("payment_intent_status_via_grpc_on_authorize_plus_kafka_projection_for_display") ∧ billing_reads_from_order("order_total_and_currency_via_grpc_authorize_request_payload_only") ∧ billing_reads_from_customer("billing_address_via_kafka_projection") ∧ fulfillment_reads_from_order("order_snapshot_via_kafka_order_confirmed_event") ∧ fulfillment_reads_from_inventory("hold_id_via_kafka_inventory_reserved_event") ∧ fulfillment_reads_from_customer("shipping_address_via_kafka_projection") ∧ inventory_reads_from_order("none_inventory_is_leaf_service") ∧ no_service_reads_another_services_tables_directly ∧ projections_stored_as_local_read_only_tables_prefixed("_proj_") ∧ grpc_for_sync_request_response_on_critical_path ∧ kafka_for_state_change_broadcasts_and_projections
events_order: order_service_publishes("order.created_v1,order.confirmed_v1,order.cancelled_v1,order.completed_v1,order.item_added_v1,order.item_removed_v1")
events_billing: billing_service_publishes("payment.intent_created_v1,payment.authorized_v1,payment.captured_v1,payment.failed_v1,payment.refunded_v1,payment.voided_v1")
events_fulfillment: fulfillment_service_publishes("shipment.created_v1,shipment.labeled_v1,shipment.dispatched_v1,shipment.delivered_v1,shipment.failed_v1")
events_inventory: inventory_service_publishes("inventory.reserved_v1,inventory.released_v1,inventory.committed_v1,stock_level.changed_v1")
events_customer: customer_service_publishes("customer.created_v1,customer.updated_v1,customer.address_changed_v1")
V: every_listed_table_has_exactly_one_owner ∧ no_foreign_key_crosses_service_boundary ∧ all_cross_service_reads_go_through_grpc_or_kafka_projection ∧ every_write_paired_with_outbox_row_in_same_tx ∧ event_schemas_registered_and_versioned_v1 ∧ projection_tables_never_written_by_business_logic_only_by_consumer ∧ ownership_doc_checked_into_repo_and_ci_lints_schema_grants
A: freeze_current_schema_snapshot ∧ annotate_each_table_with_owner_label_in_migration_metadata ∧ introduce_per_service_postgres_role_with_grants_only_on_owned_tables ∧ add_outbox_table_per_service ∧ publish_v1_event_contracts_to_schema_registry ∧ build_projection_consumers_for_customer_billing_inventory_in_order_and_fulfillment ∧ replace_in_process_joins_with_grpc_calls_on_checkout_path ∧ add_ci_check_rejecting_cross_schema_references ∧ document_ownership_matrix_in_architecture_md

@flint v0 hybrid
G: design_order_to_payment_saga_state_machine_idempotent_with_compensations_and_timeouts
C: no_2pc ∧ no_distributed_locks ∧ orchestrator_lives_in_order_service ∧ saga_instance_row_per_order ∧ every_step_keyed_by_idempotency_key("order_id+step_name") ∧ outbox_for_all_sends ∧ inbox_for_all_consumes ∧ grpc_for_sync_reserve_and_authorize ∧ kafka_for_capture_and_fulfillment_progress
states: draft ∧ reserving_inventory ∧ inventory_reserved ∧ authorizing_payment ∧ payment_authorized ∧ confirmed ∧ capturing_payment ∧ payment_captured ∧ fulfilling ∧ fulfilled ∧ completed ∧ compensating ∧ cancelled ∧ failed_terminal
transitions: draft_to_reserving_on("checkout_submitted") ∧ reserving_to_inventory_reserved_on("inventory.reserve_ok") ∧ reserving_to_compensating_on("inventory.reserve_denied_or_timeout") ∧ inventory_reserved_to_authorizing_on("internal_next") ∧ authorizing_to_payment_authorized_on("billing.authorize_ok") ∧ authorizing_to_compensating_on("billing.authorize_failed_or_timeout") ∧ payment_authorized_to_confirmed_on("internal_next_emit_order_confirmed") ∧ confirmed_to_capturing_on("fulfillment.dispatched_v1") ∧ capturing_to_payment_captured_on("payment.captured_v1") ∧ capturing_to_compensating_on("payment.capture_failed_after_max_retry") ∧ payment_captured_to_fulfilling_on("already_dispatched_marker") ∧ fulfilling_to_fulfilled_on("shipment.delivered_v1") ∧ fulfilled_to_completed_on("internal_finalize") ∧ any_nonterminal_to_cancelled_on("user_cancel_before_dispatch")
compensations: reserving_failed_compensation("mark_order_failed_no_side_effects") ∧ authorizing_failed_compensation("publish_inventory.release_cmd_v1_then_mark_failed") ∧ confirmed_but_capture_failed_compensation("publish_shipment.cancel_cmd_v1_then_inventory.release_cmd_v1_then_billing.void_auth_cmd_v1_then_mark_cancelled") ∧ partial_fulfillment_rollback("per_shipment_reversal_emit_billing.refund_cmd_v1_for_undelivered_lines_plus_inventory.restock_cmd_v1") ∧ inventory_released_after_payment_recovery("billing.refund_cmd_v1_plus_notify_customer_plus_mark_cancelled") ∧ all_compensations_idempotent_via_compensation_key("order_id+step_name+attempt")
timeouts: reserve_timeout("8s_grpc_then_saga_retry_max_3_then_compensate") ∧ authorize_timeout("10s_grpc_then_saga_retry_max_2_then_compensate") ∧ capture_timeout("5min_await_payment.captured_then_retry_capture_cmd_max_5_with_exp_backoff") ∧ fulfillment_timeout("72h_no_dispatch_then_alert_plus_cancel_with_void_auth") ∧ delivery_timeout("14d_no_delivered_then_open_carrier_investigation_not_auto_cancel") ∧ saga_heartbeat_scanner_every("30s_finds_stuck_sagas_by_state_plus_updated_at")
idempotency: every_command_carries_idempotency_key ∧ every_handler_checks_inbox_before_apply ∧ state_transitions_use_compare_and_set_on_current_state_plus_version ∧ kafka_consumer_offsets_committed_after_db_tx ∧ duplicate_events_no_op ∧ compensations_safe_to_replay
critical_scenarios: payment_timeout_handled_by("capture_cmd_retry_then_void_auth_then_compensate") ∧ inventory_released_after_payment_handled_by("detect_via_inventory.released_v1_while_state_in_authorized_or_later_then_refund_and_cancel_and_page_oncage") ∧ partial_fulfillment_rollback_handled_by("shipment_scoped_compensation_not_order_scoped_refund_only_undelivered_lines_keep_delivered_lines_captured") ∧ double_authorize_prevented_by("idempotency_key_on_billing.authorize_plus_one_active_payment_intent_per_order") ∧ orphan_reservation_prevented_by("reservation_ttl_15min_plus_inventory_self_expiry_plus_order_refresh_heartbeat_until_confirmed")
V: saga_has_exactly_one_active_state_per_order_at_all_times ∧ replaying_any_event_or_command_yields_same_final_state ∧ no_step_holds_a_lock_across_service_boundary ∧ every_terminal_state_leaves_zero_held_funds_and_zero_held_stock ∧ chaos_tests_pass("kill_billing_mid_authorize,kill_inventory_mid_reserve,kafka_duplicate_delivery,kafka_out_of_order,clock_skew_5min") ∧ recovery_rto_under_5min_for_stuck_saga ∧ audit_log_reconstructs_full_order_history_from_state_log_plus_events
A: add_saga_instances_table_with_state_version_and_last_event_id ∧ add_inbox_table_per_service_keyed_by_message_id ∧ implement_orchestrator_as_event_driven_handler_not_long_running_thread ∧ wire_heartbeat_scanner_cronjob_in_k8s ∧ define_command_topics_and_reply_topics_with_correlation_id ∧ ship_chaos_test_suite_before_cutover ∧ add_saga_state_dashboard_plus_stuck_saga_alert ∧ document_state_machine_diagram_in_architecture_md

@flint v0 hybrid
G: identify_dangerous_eventual_consistency_windows_and_bound_each_with_mitigation
C: saga_driven_order_to_payment ∧ kafka_projections_for_cross_service_reads ∧ grpc_on_critical_path ∧ user_facing_surfaces("checkout,order_history,order_detail,receipt_email,inventory_pdp,admin_console,refund_ui") ∧ operators_and_customers_both_affected
window_1_order_history_lag: problem("order.confirmed_v1_to_order_history_projection_in_read_api") ∧ risk("user_pays_then_sees_empty_history_files_duplicate_order") ∧ bound("p99_under_500ms_hard_ceiling_2s") ∧ mitigation("read_your_writes_via_session_pinned_read_from_primary_for_60s_plus_client_optimistic_insert_with_order_id_from_confirm_response_plus_spinner_until_projection_lag_header_clears")
window_2_payment_status_display: problem("payment.authorized_v1_to_billing_projection_on_order_detail") ∧ risk("customer_sees_pending_after_successful_pay_then_retries_payment") ∧ bound("p99_under_1s") ∧ mitigation("order_service_returns_authoritative_payment_intent_status_via_grpc_on_detail_fetch_fallback_to_projection_only_if_grpc_down_plus_ui_shows_processing_never_failed_until_t_plus_10s")
window_3_inventory_pdp_stock_count: problem("stock_level.changed_v1_to_pdp_read_cache") ∧ risk("oversell_illusion_or_ghost_out_of_stock") ∧ bound("p99_under_2s_for_display_hard_5s") ∧ mitigation("pdp_shows_banded_availability_in_stock_low_stock_out_of_stock_not_exact_count_plus_authoritative_reserve_call_at_add_to_cart_plus_ttl_reservation_plus_never_trust_cache_at_checkout")
window_4_inventory_reserved_but_order_not_yet_visible: problem("reservation_held_15min_but_order_row_not_yet_projected_to_admin") ∧ risk("support_sees_no_order_cannot_help_customer") ∧ bound("admin_sees_within_1s_of_reserve_ok") ∧ mitigation("admin_console_reads_orders_from_order_service_directly_via_grpc_not_from_projection_plus_show_saga_state_badge")
window_5_refund_appears_in_ledger: problem("payment.refunded_v1_to_billing_ledger_projection_and_order_detail") ∧ risk("customer_sees_captured_after_refund_initiated_calls_support_twice") ∧ bound("p99_under_2s_user_facing_under_10s_email") ∧ mitigation("refund_ui_returns_refund_id_immediately_plus_order_detail_merges_local_pending_refund_marker_until_projection_catches_up_plus_receipt_email_gated_on_projection_lag_lt_5s")
window_6_shipment_dispatched_vs_payment_captured_ordering: problem("shipment.dispatched_v1_triggers_capture_but_capture_ack_lags") ∧ risk("customer_sees_shipped_but_not_charged_doubts_legitimacy") ∧ bound("capture_ack_visible_under_5s_of_dispatch_email") ∧ mitigation("do_not_send_shipped_email_until_payment_captured_projection_confirms_or_timeout_5s_then_send_with_processing_payment_copy")
window_7_cancel_race_with_dispatch: problem("user_clicks_cancel_while_fulfillment_dispatches_concurrently") ∧ risk("cancel_accepted_ui_then_package_ships_anyway") ∧ bound("cancel_decision_authoritative_within_2s") ∧ mitigation("cancel_endpoint_is_synchronous_grpc_to_order_service_which_compare_and_sets_state_plus_emits_cancel_cmd_only_if_pre_dispatch_plus_ui_shows_cancellation_pending_never_cancelled_until_ack")
window_8_customer_address_change_propagation: problem("customer.address_changed_v1_to_fulfillment_projection_mid_checkout") ∧ risk("ships_to_old_address_after_user_updated_it") ∧ bound("address_snapshot_locked_at_order_confirmed_not_eventually_consistent") ∧ mitigation("order_service_snapshots_shipping_address_into_order_row_at_checkout_via_grpc_read_fulfillment_reads_order_snapshot_not_customer_projection")
window_9_saga_state_vs_published_event: problem("order.confirmed_v1_committed_to_db_but_outbox_relay_lag") ∧ risk("downstream_services_lag_user_refreshes_sees_stale_state") ∧ bound("outbox_relay_p99_under_200ms") ∧ mitigation("outbox_relay_runs_in_tight_loop_plus_notify_listen_on_outbox_insert_plus_lag_alert_at_1s_plus_ui_poll_falls_back_to_order_service_grpc_not_projection")
window_10_kafka_consumer_rebalance: problem("consumer_rebalance_pauses_projection_30s_plus") ∧ risk("multi_window_blast_radius_order_history_payment_status_stock_all_stale_simultaneously") ∧ bound("rebalance_under_10s_alert_over_30s") ∧ mitigation("sticky_partition_assignor_plus_static_member_ids_plus_readiness_probe_gates_traffic_plus_ui_shows_degraded_banner_when_projection_lag_header_exceeds_threshold")
V: every_user_visible_surface_has_declared_bound_and_mitigation ∧ projection_lag_exposed_as_header_x_projection_lag_ms_on_all_read_apis ∧ critical_paths_checkout_and_cancel_and_refund_use_authoritative_grpc_not_projection ∧ slo_dashboard_tracks_each_window_p50_p99_breach_count ∧ synthetic_probe_exercises_all_ten_windows_every_minute ∧ no_email_sent_on_eventually_consistent_state_without_lag_gate
A: instrument_projection_lag_per_topic_per_consumer_group ∧ add_x_projection_lag_ms_response_header_to_read_apis ∧ add_read_your_writes_session_pin_to_gateway ∧ snapshot_address_and_pricing_into_order_row_at_checkout ∧ switch_admin_console_and_cancel_and_refund_to_grpc_authoritative_reads ∧ gate_transactional_emails_on_lag_threshold ∧ ship_ui_degraded_banner_tied_to_lag_header ∧ document_consistency_contract_per_surface_in_architecture_md

Over the next six months we are splitting our orders platform from a single monolithic application into four focused services aligned to how the business actually works: orders, billing, fulfillment, and inventory. Today, all four domains share one codebase and one database, which means every team's changes collide at merge time, releases have to be coordinated across the whole system, and a twelve-minute test suite runs on every small change. By giving each domain its own service, database schema, and deployment pipeline, we expect faster and safer releases, fewer cross-team merge conflicts, shorter feedback loops for engineers, and the ability for each team to move at its own pace without waiting on the others. The customer-facing order-to-payment flow will continue to behave as a single, strongly consistent transaction from the user's perspective, preserved through a carefully designed coordination pattern rather than through database-level locking.

The principal risk is that distributed systems introduce short windows where different parts of the product can temporarily disagree with one another, for example an order taking a fraction of a second to appear in a customer's history after checkout. We have identified each of these windows, set explicit bounds on how long they are allowed to last, and designed mitigations so that customers never see a confusing or contradictory state on the critical paths of checkout, cancellation, and refund. The migration will proceed one service at a time behind the existing system, so at every stage we can fall back to the current platform if something goes wrong. Months one through three carve out inventory, fulfillment, and billing; months four and five reshape the remaining order service and retire the monolith; month six is reserved for hardening, failure testing, and a controlled cutover. The work is sized for the current team of eight engineers and sits comfortably inside our two-year runway.

@flint v0 hybrid
G: define_five_day_one_slos_with_thresholds_canary_queries_and_dashboard_groupings
C: post_cutover_day_one ∧ canary_cohort_percent("10") ∧ control_cohort_percent("90") ∧ stack("prometheus+grafana+kafka_lag_exporter+otel_traces") ∧ comparison_window("rolling_30min") ∧ baseline_from_pre_cutover_14d_median ∧ alert_routing("pagerduty_sev2_plus_slack_orders_oncall")
slo_1_order_to_payment_success_rate: metric("order_confirmed_count_div_checkout_submitted_count") ∧ target("99.5_percent") ∧ warn_threshold("99.3_for_10min") ∧ page_threshold("99.0_for_5min_or_canary_minus_control_gt_0.3pp") ∧ canary_query("rate(order_confirmed_total{cohort=canary}[5m])/rate(checkout_submitted_total{cohort=canary}[5m])_vs_same_with_cohort_control") ∧ dashboard_group("business_health")
slo_2_order_to_payment_latency_p99: metric("checkout_submit_to_order_confirmed_duration_seconds") ∧ target("p99_under_500ms_p50_under_120ms") ∧ warn_threshold("p99_over_600ms_for_10min") ∧ page_threshold("p99_over_1s_for_5min_or_canary_p99_gt_1.5x_control_p99") ∧ canary_query("histogram_quantile(0.99,sum_by_le_cohort_rate_bucket_5m)_compare_cohorts") ∧ dashboard_group("critical_path_latency")
slo_3_saga_stuck_rate: metric("count_saga_instances_where_state_not_terminal_and_updated_at_older_than_5min") ∧ target("under_0.1_percent_of_active_sagas") ∧ warn_threshold("over_10_stuck_sagas_for_10min") ∧ page_threshold("over_50_stuck_or_over_1_percent_of_active_for_5min") ∧ canary_query("sum_by_cohort_stuck_sagas_div_active_sagas") ∧ dashboard_group("saga_health")
slo_4_kafka_consumer_lag_on_critical_projections: metric("kafka_consumergroup_lag_seconds{topic=order.confirmed_v1|payment.authorized_v1|inventory.reserved_v1}") ∧ target("p99_under_500ms") ∧ warn_threshold("p99_over_1s_for_10min") ∧ page_threshold("p99_over_5s_for_5min_or_any_topic_lag_over_30s") ∧ canary_query("max_by_topic_cohort_lag_seconds") ∧ dashboard_group("event_bus_health")
slo_5_payment_authorization_grpc_success_and_latency: metric("billing.authorize_rpc_success_rate_and_duration") ∧ target("success_99.9_percent_p99_under_300ms") ∧ warn_threshold("success_under_99.7_or_p99_over_400ms_for_10min") ∧ page_threshold("success_under_99.5_for_5min_or_canary_minus_control_success_gt_0.2pp_or_p99_gt_1.5x_control") ∧ canary_query("sum_rate_grpc_server_handled_total_code_ok_by_cohort_div_total_by_cohort_plus_histogram_quantile_p99_duration") ∧ dashboard_group("sync_rpc_health")
canary_vs_control_method: cohort_label_propagated_via_trace_baggage_and_request_header ∧ metrics_tagged_cohort("canary|control") ∧ comparison_expression("canary_metric_minus_control_metric_over_pooled_stddev") ∧ auto_rollback_trigger("any_slo_page_threshold_held_for_5min_in_canary_cohort") ∧ dashboard_overlay_canary_and_control_on_same_panel_with_delta_annotation
dashboard_groupings: business_health("success_rate,volume,revenue_per_min") ∧ critical_path_latency("checkout_p50_p99,grpc_p99_per_hop,saga_step_duration") ∧ saga_health("active_by_state,stuck_count,compensation_rate,retry_rate") ∧ event_bus_health("lag_per_topic,consumer_rebalance_count,projection_lag_header_p99,dlq_depth") ∧ sync_rpc_health("success_per_service,p99_per_route,timeout_rate,circuit_breaker_state") ∧ infra_overlay("pod_cpu_mem,hpa_replicas,pg_conn_pool_saturation,redis_hit_rate")
V: each_slo_has_target_warn_page_and_canary_query ∧ every_dashboard_panel_supports_cohort_split ∧ auto_rollback_wired_to_canary_breach ∧ runbook_linked_from_every_alert ∧ day_one_review_at_t_plus_24h_checks_all_five_slos_green_for_full_window
A: deploy_cohort_label_injection_at_gateway ∧ ship_five_grafana_panels_with_canary_overlay_before_cutover ∧ wire_alert_rules_with_two_tier_warn_and_page ∧ enable_auto_rollback_hook_on_argo_rollouts ∧ publish_runbooks_for_each_slo_breach ∧ schedule_day_one_review_meeting_at_t_plus_24h

@flint v0 hybrid
G: audit_jwt_module_rank_severity
C: hs256_and_rs256_and_none_all_accepted ∧ verify_exp_disabled ∧ secret_hardcoded_in_source ∧ algo_taken_from_untrusted_header ∧ role_default_user_on_missing_claim ∧ no_iss_aud_nbf_iat_kid_checks ∧ no_signature_algo_binding_to_key_type
P: critical_alg_none_accepted_bypasses_signature ∧ critical_algo_confusion_rs256_verified_as_hs256_with_public_key_as_secret ∧ critical_hardcoded_dev_secret_in_repo ∧ high_expiration_disabled_tokens_never_expire ∧ high_unverified_header_drives_decode_algorithm ∧ medium_role_defaults_to_user_but_no_allowlist_or_claim_required ∧ medium_missing_iss_aud_validation_enables_cross_service_replay ∧ low_no_kid_or_key_rotation_support ∧ low_no_nbf_iat_leeway_control
V: call_verify_with_alg_none_token_must_raise ∧ call_verify_with_rs256_token_signed_by_public_key_must_raise ∧ call_verify_with_expired_token_must_raise ∧ secret_loaded_from_env_or_kms_not_literal ∧ algorithms_arg_equals_single_value_list ∧ require_claims_sub_exp_iss_aud
A: drop_none_and_rs256_from_algos_keep_only("HS256") ∧ remove_get_unverified_header_branch ∧ pass_algorithms_as_fixed_list_to_decode ∧ enable_verify_exp_and_verify_signature ∧ load_secret_from_env("JWT_SECRET") ∧ require_issuer_and_audience("iss","aud") ∧ fail_closed_if_role_claim_missing ∧ add_leeway_and_nbf_iat_checks ∧ add_key_rotation_via_kid

@flint v0 hybrid
G: rank_top_3_decode_path_attacks_with_payloads
C: decode_accepts_algos_list_including_none_hs256_rs256 ∧ algo_chosen_from_unverified_header ∧ verify_exp_false ∧ secret_is_known_literal ∧ role_claim_drives_authorization

Fix: drop "none" + RS256 from accepted algos, load secret from env, pin `algorithms=["HS256"]` in decode, enforce exp/iss/aud on new tokens. Grandfather: tokens with `iat < cutoff` skip iss/aud requirement but still verify HS256 signature + exp. New `sign()` stamps iss/aud/iat. `verify()` signature unchanged.

```python
# auth/jwt_service.py
import os
import time
import jwt
from dataclasses import dataclass

SECRET = os.environ["JWT_SECRET"]
ISSUER = os.environ.get("JWT_ISSUER", "sigil-auth")
AUDIENCE = os.environ.get("JWT_AUDIENCE", "sigil-api")
ALGORITHM = "HS256"

# Grandfather: tokens issued before this cutoff may lack iss/aud claims.
# Cutoff = fix deploy time; 30-day window enforced via exp on old tokens.
GRANDFATHER_CUTOFF = int(os.environ.get("JWT_GRANDFATHER_CUTOFF", "0"))
GRANDFATHER_WINDOW = 30 * 24 * 3600

ALLOWED_ROLES = {"user", "admin"}


@dataclass
class Token:
    sub: str
    exp: int
    role: str


def sign(sub: str, role: str, ttl: int = 3600) -> str:
    if role not in ALLOWED_ROLES:
        raise ValueError("bad role")
    now = int(time.time())
    payload = {
        "sub": sub,
        "role": role,
        "iat": now,
        "nbf": now,
        "exp": now + ttl,
        "iss": ISSUER,
        "aud": AUDIENCE,
    }
    return jwt.encode(payload, SECRET, algorithm=ALGORITHM)


def verify(token: str) -> Token:
    # Peek iat to decide grandfather path. Signature still verified below.
    unverified = jwt.decode(
        token,
        SECRET,
        algorithms=[ALGORITHM],
        options={"verify_exp": False, "verify_aud": False, "verify_iss": False},
        leeway=30,
    )
    iat = int(unverified.get("iat", 0))
    now = int(time.time())

    is_grandfathered = (
        GRANDFATHER_CUTOFF > 0
        and 0 < iat < GRANDFATHER_CUTOFF
        and (now - iat) < GRANDFATHER_WINDOW
    )

    if is_grandfathered:
        payload = jwt.decode(
            token,
            SECRET,
            algorithms=[ALGORITHM],
            options={"require": ["exp", "sub"]},
            leeway=30,
        )
    else:
        payload = jwt.decode(
            token,
            SECRET,
            algorithms=[ALGORITHM],
            audience=AUDIENCE,
            issuer=ISSUER,
            options={"require": ["exp", "iat", "sub", "iss", "aud"]},
            leeway=30,
        )

    role = payload.get("role")
    if role not in ALLOWED_ROLES:
        raise PermissionError("missing or invalid role claim")

    return Token(sub=payload["sub"], exp=int(payload["exp"]), role=role)


def require_admin(token_str: str) -> Token:
    t = verify(token_str)
    if t.role != "admin":
        raise PermissionError("not admin")
    return t
```

Tests exercise decode path. Use env setup via autouse fixture so module loads with known secret. Cover 5 vectors: `alg=none`, RS256 mismatch, expired, forged signature (wrong secret), role tamper (re-encode payload, reuse old signature).

```python
# tests/test_jwt_service.py
import base64
import importlib
import json
import os
import time

import jwt
import pytest
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import rsa

SECRET = "unit-test-secret-kXr8"
WRONG_SECRET = "attacker-guess"
ISSUER = "sigil-auth"
AUDIENCE = "sigil-api"


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def jwt_env(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_SECRET", SECRET)
    monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_ISSUER", ISSUER)
    monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_AUDIENCE", AUDIENCE)
    monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_GRANDFATHER_CUTOFF", str(int(time.time()) - 3600))
    import auth.jwt_service as svc
    importlib.reload(svc)
    return svc


def _b64url(data: bytes) -> str:
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(data).rstrip(b"=").decode()


def _unsigned(header: dict, payload: dict) -> str:
    h = _b64url(json.dumps(header, separators=(",", ":")).encode())
    p = _b64url(json.dumps(payload, separators=(",", ":")).encode())
    return f"{h}.{p}"


def test_alg_none_rejected(jwt_env):
    now = int(time.time())
    token = _unsigned(
        {"alg": "none", "typ": "JWT"},
        {"sub": "attacker", "role": "admin", "exp": now + 3600,
         "iat": now, "iss": ISSUER, "aud": AUDIENCE},
    ) + "."
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        jwt_env.verify(token)


def test_rs256_token_rejected(jwt_env):
    key = rsa.generate_private_key(public_exponent=65537, key_size=2048)
    now = int(time.time())
    token = jwt.encode(
        {"sub": "attacker", "role": "admin", "exp": now + 3600,
         "iat": now, "iss": ISSUER, "aud": AUDIENCE},
        key,
        algorithm="RS256",
    )
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        jwt_env.verify(token)


def test_expired_token_rejected(jwt_env):
    now = int(time.time())
    token = jwt.encode(
        {"sub": "u", "role": "user", "exp": now - 60,
         "iat": now - 3600, "iss": ISSUER, "aud": AUDIENCE},
        SECRET,
        algorithm="HS256",
    )
    with pytest.raises(jwt.ExpiredSignatureError):
        jwt_env.verify(token)


def test_forged_signature_rejected(jwt_env):
    now = int(time.time())
    token = jwt.encode(
        {"sub": "attacker", "role": "admin", "exp": now + 3600,
         "iat": now, "iss": ISSUER, "aud": AUDIENCE},
        WRONG_SECRET,
        algorithm="HS256",
    )
    with pytest.raises(jwt.InvalidSignatureError):
        jwt_env.verify(token)


def test_role_spoof_via_payload_tamper_rejected(jwt_env):
    good = jwt_env.sign("victim", "user", ttl=3600)
    header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64 = good.split(".")
    padded = payload_b64 + "=" * (-len(payload_b64) % 4)
    claims = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded))
    claims["role"] = "admin"
    tampered_payload = _b64url(json.dumps(claims, separators=(",", ":")).encode())
    tampered = f"{header_b64}.{tampered_payload}.{sig_b64}"
    with pytest.raises(jwt.InvalidSignatureError):
        jwt_env.require_admin(tampered)


def test_valid_token_roundtrip(jwt_env):
    token = jwt_env.sign("alice", "admin", ttl=3600)
    t = jwt_env.require_admin(token)
    assert t.sub == "alice"
    assert t.role == "admin"
```

Subject: Security findings in our JWT authentication module and recommended path forward

During a routine review of the authentication service, the security team identified a set of serious weaknesses in how the module validates user login tokens. In plain terms, the code that is supposed to confirm a user's identity and permission level was not doing so reliably. An attacker familiar with these weaknesses could craft a token that the system would accept as valid, including one that claims administrator privileges, without ever compromising a real user account or password. The issues stem from overly permissive settings, a signing key that was committed to source control, and verification logic that trusted information an attacker controls.

The business risk is material. Administrator-level access would allow an attacker to read, modify, or delete customer data, adjust account permissions, and perform actions that appear in our logs as legitimate internal activity. Because the required attack payloads are well-documented in public security literature and do not require specialized tooling, we should assume that any sufficiently motivated party who obtained a sample token, or who read the relevant source, could execute the attack. We have no evidence at this time that any such exploitation has occurred, and we are reviewing authentication logs to confirm that assessment.

A fix is ready and has been tested against the specific attack patterns identified. It restricts token validation to a single strong algorithm, moves the signing key out of source and into our secrets manager, enforces token expiration, and preserves a thirty-day grandfather window so that existing customer sessions are not disrupted. We recommend deploying the fix within the next business day. In parallel, we will rotate the signing key so that any token issued under the old configuration is retired, and we will add monitoring to flag any further attempts to use weak token formats.

On disclosure, our current read is that mandatory customer or regulator notification is not triggered absent evidence of actual access to personal data, and the log review now underway is the gating item. We will escalate immediately if that review surfaces anything consistent with exploitation. Legal and Privacy are being looped in today so that, should the picture change, we are prepared to meet the relevant notification timelines without delay.

## Incident Post-Mortem: Checkout Errors on April 20

On the evening of April 20, our checkout service experienced three brief periods of degraded availability between 22:04 and 22:50 UTC. During each window, a portion of requests to the checkout endpoint returned server errors or timed out before completing. The total customer-impacting time was approximately 23 minutes across the three windows, with each individual disruption lasting between three and five seconds longer than it should have, and recurring roughly every 23 minutes until we intervened. Checkouts that failed during these windows were not charged, and no order data was lost or corrupted.

The impact was concentrated on customers who attempted to complete a purchase during the affected minutes. Those customers would have seen an error page or a spinning checkout button, and would have needed to retry. Browsing, account access, and all other parts of the product remained fully functional throughout the incident. Our payment provider, inventory, and downstream systems were unaffected, and no payment was double-charged or partially processed as a result of this event.

The root cause was an interaction between two parts of our own infrastructure rather than a failure of any single component. A routine background job that cleans up expired user sessions shares the same pool of database connections as the live checkout service. On the night of the incident, the cleanup job had grown large enough that each run temporarily consumed enough of that shared resource to leave checkout requests waiting in line. When the wait exceeded our five-second safety threshold, those requests were cancelled and surfaced as errors. The job itself was behaving correctly; the problem was that two very different kinds of work were competing for the same lane.

We have already deployed a fix that gives the cleanup job its own dedicated, tightly capped connection pool, so it can no longer compete with checkout traffic regardless of how much data it processes. We also restructured the cleanup to work in small, quickly-committed batches and added a database index that makes each batch substantially faster. Together, these changes make a recurrence of this specific failure mode structurally very unlikely, not merely less frequent.

Beyond the immediate fix, we are auditing our other background jobs for the same class of resource sharing, and we are adding alerting that will warn us when the checkout connection pool approaches saturation well before any customer sees an error. We are sorry for the disruption this caused, particularly to customers whose purchases were interrupted. If you believe you were affected and would like us to look into a specific order, our support team is ready to help and has full context on the incident.

Looking back, the failure was not really a bug in any single piece of code. It was a property of how two systems shared a resource, and that kind of property is almost invisible when you review a pull request in isolation. The reaper was written correctly; the checkout handler was written correctly; the connection pool was sized reasonably for either workload on its own. What we lacked was any review gate that asked the question our incident asked for us, which is what happens when these pieces run against each other under load. A lightweight architectural review for any change that introduces a new scheduled job or batch process, specifically focused on what shared infrastructure it touches and what it holds for how long, would almost certainly have surfaced the shared-pool concern the first time the reaper's scan size started growing.

The second gap was in our testing posture. Our load tests exercise the checkout path in isolation against a clean database, which is exactly the condition under which the system never fails. We do not routinely simulate background work running concurrently with foreground traffic, and we do not run load tests against a database whose size and session-table depth resemble production. If we had a staging profile that reproduced production's table sizes and ran the scheduled jobs on their real cadence, the pool saturation would have shown up as a p99 spike long before a customer ever saw it. This is a worthwhile investment independent of this incident, because the same class of problem will keep finding us in other jobs until we close it structurally.

The third reflection is about observability, and it is the one I find most useful to sit with. We had the data to see this coming. Connection pool utilization is a metric we already emit, and the reaper's runtime has been creeping up for months. What we did not have was an alert that fired on the leading indicator rather than the trailing one; we learned about saturation from customer-facing error rates instead of from the pool itself. Committing to alert on resource pressure before it becomes user-visible pressure, and treating a sustained rise in a leading metric as something worth investigating even when nothing is broken yet, is the habit that would have turned this from an incident into a quiet Tuesday ticket.
